explanationZhTw: 這裡是形容詞比較級，表示「狀況更好、恢復得更好」。
generatedExample: She felt much better after resting for a day.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是指植物生長，而是指「變得、逐漸成為」；整句是在說他一天比一天更胖也更強壯。
generatedExample: The sky was growing darker before the storm.

explanationZhTw: 表示「合適的、恰當的」。這裡是在質疑把那種狀態稱作「天」是否算合適。
generatedExample: It is proper to wait your turn in line.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `cell` 不是「細胞」，而是「牢房」；用在監禁環境中。
generatedExample: He spent the night in a cold cell with no window.

explanationZhTw: `plank bed` 指木板床，強調床面很硬、很簡陋。
generatedExample: The cabin had a plank bed and a rough blanket.

explanationZhTw: `stool` 在這裡是「凳子、無靠背的椅子」。
generatedExample: She sat on a stool beside the kitchen counter.

explanationZhTw: `tin basin` 是「錫製臉盆／洗臉盆」，屬於很簡單但具體的名詞片語。
generatedExample: We washed the vegetables in a tin basin.

explanationZhTw: `underclothes` 是「內衣、貼身衣物」的總稱。
generatedExample: Pack extra underclothes for the trip.

explanationZhTw: `suit of overalls` 指一套工作服／連身工作褲，這裡是整套衣服。
generatedExample: The mechanic changed into a suit of overalls.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `dressed` 不是「穿衣服」，而是「包紮、處理傷口」；`varicose ulcer` 是靜脈曲張潰瘍。
generatedExample: The nurse dressed the wound with clean gauze.

explanationZhTw: `pulled out` 是片語動詞，這裡指「拔除、拔掉」。
generatedExample: The dentist pulled out my damaged tooth.

explanationZhTw: `remnants` 指「殘餘物、剩下的部分」；這裡是牙齒剩下的殘片。
generatedExample: Only remnants of the old bridge were left after the storm.

explanationZhTw: `dentures` 是「假牙」，通常指一整副可拆卸的牙齒替代品。
generatedExample: My grandfather keeps his dentures in a glass at night.

explanationZhTw: 「keep count」在這裡是指「持續記數、追蹤」，也就是留意時間經過了多久。
generatedExample: She tried to keep count of how many pills she had taken.

explanationZhTw: 「regular intervals」指「固定間隔、規律的時間間隔」，這裡是在說食物大約按照固定時間送來。
generatedExample: The train arrives at regular intervals during rush hour.

explanationZhTw: 「dimly」在這裡不是指光線微弱，而是指「模糊地、隱約地」思考或感覺。
generatedExample: I dimly remembered meeting her years ago.

explanationZhTw: 「persevered」表示「堅持下去、鍥而不捨」，即使一開始不舒服，他還是繼續嘗試。
generatedExample: She persevered with the course until she finally passed.

explanationZhTw: 「spun ... out」在這裡是「把……延長使用、盡量撐久一點」的意思，表示他很省著抽那包煙。
generatedExample: He spun the snacks out so they would last the whole trip.

explanationZhTw: 「torpid」指遲鈍、沒精神、幾乎動不了；這裡形容他即使清醒也非常虛弱、麻木。
generatedExample: The medicine made him feel torpid for the rest of the day.

explanationZhTw: 「vague reveries」是模糊的白日夢或出神幻想；reveries 指飄忽的幻想，不是清楚的思考。
generatedExample: During the train ride, she drifted into vague reveries about childhood summers.

explanationZhTw: 「grown used to」表示已經習慣了；指經過一段時間後，某種情況不再讓人覺得奇怪或不舒服。
generatedExample: After a month in the city, he had grown used to the constant traffic noise.

explanationZhTw: 「coherent」是連貫的、清楚有條理的；這裡說夢變得比較有脈絡、較容易理解。
generatedExample: Her argument was coherent and easy to follow.

explanationZhTw: 「intellectual effort」指腦力上的努力，也就是需要思考、分析和專注的精神用力。
generatedExample: The exam required more intellectual effort than he expected.

explanationZhTw: 「stimulus」是刺激、促動因素；這裡指痛苦原本像一種驅動力量，讓他保持警醒或有反應。
generatedExample: A clear goal can be a strong stimulus for better performance.

explanationZhTw: 「distraction」在這裡是能讓人轉移注意力的事物，不是字面上的「分心動作」。
generatedExample: She turned off her phone to avoid distraction while studying.

explanationZhTw: 「satisfying」是令人滿足的；這裡表示只要不受虐待、能吃飽、保持乾淨，就已經足夠了。
generatedExample: A simple warm meal felt deeply satisfying after the long hike.

explanationZhTw: 「impulse」指一時的衝動或念頭；這裡是指他完全沒有想要起床的衝動。
generatedExample: I had the impulse to call her, but I waited until morning.

explanationZhTw: 「established beyond a doubt」是慣用語，意思是已經被完全證實、毫無疑問。
generatedExample: The test results established beyond a doubt that the water was contaminated.

explanationZhTw: 「reluctantly」表示勉強地、不情願地；這裡是說他一開始很不願意運動。
generatedExample: She reluctantly agreed to join the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「pacing」在這裡不是「節奏」，而是指來回走動來量距離。
generatedExample: He spent the afternoon pacing the room while he waited for news.

explanationZhTw: 「bowed」在這裡指彎曲、駝著的；「bowed shoulders」就是駝肩、含胸的肩膀姿勢。
generatedExample: After months at the desk, his bowed shoulders started to hurt.

explanationZhTw: 「elaborate」表示更複雜、更精細的；這裡是指他嘗試更難的運動。
generatedExample: They planned an elaborate celebration for her birthday.

explanationZhTw: 「at arm's length」是慣用語，意思是伸直手臂的距離；這裡用來描述把凳子伸遠。
generatedExample: Keep the map at arm's length so you can read the directions clearly.

explanationZhTw: 「falling over」是片語動詞，意思是失去平衡而跌倒。
generatedExample: The child kept falling over on the slippery floor.

explanationZhTw: 「feat」指艱難完成的事、壯舉或了不起的成就。
generatedExample: Finishing the marathon in that heat was an impressive feat.

explanationZhTw: 「chanced to」在這裡表示「碰巧、偶然地做了某事」，語氣比一般的「happen to」更文學。
generatedExample: I chanced to meet my old teacher at the station.

explanationZhTw: 「set to work」表示開始認真動手做某事；在這裡是指他開始專心進行自我改造。
generatedExample: She set to work on the report as soon as she got to the office.

explanationZhTw: 「deliberately」表示刻意地、故意地、很有意識地；這裡強調他不是隨便想想，而是有目的地進行。
generatedExample: He deliberately chose the quieter path home.

explanationZhTw: 「re-educating himself」在這裡不是一般的再教育課程，而是指他試圖重新訓練自己的思想與觀念，帶有政治或意識形態上的自我改造意味。
generatedExample: After the move, he spent months re-educating himself about the local rules and customs.

explanationZhTw: 已經屈服、投降；在這裡表示他承認自己已經認輸。
generatedExample: After hours of pressure, he had capitulated and signed the statement.

explanationZhTw: 早就準備要屈服；表示他其實很早以前就已經動搖。
generatedExample: She was ready to capitulate once she realized the deadline could not be moved.

explanationZhTw: 輕率、輕佻；這裡指他那種反抗行動在事後看來很可笑、很不切實際。
generatedExample: He treated the matter with frivolity, but everyone else was serious.

explanationZhTw: 膚淺、空洞；此處指他的反抗沒有深度，也沒有真正成功的可能。
generatedExample: The shallowness of the argument became obvious after a few questions.

explanationZhTw: 與某個強大力量對抗；這裡帶有「自不量力地挑戰」的語氣。
generatedExample: He knew it was foolish to set himself up against the company alone.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的特定名詞，指監視、控制思想的秘密警察。
generatedExample: The novel uses the Thought Police as a symbol of total surveillance.

explanationZhTw: 推斷、推論；根據線索得出結論。
generatedExample: From the broken window, they could infer that someone had entered the room.

explanationZhTw: 是對的、站在正確的一方；這是慣用說法，不是字面上的「在右邊」。
generatedExample: Even after the debate, she still believed she was in the right.

explanationZhTw: 集體大腦；這裡是比喻，指黨被說成像一個共享智慧、無法犯錯的整體。
generatedExample: The manager spoke as if the team were a collective brain that never made errors.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是反常且帶諷刺的用法：把「正常」說成由統計決定，暗示多數人認同就算正常。
generatedExample: In his world, truth was statistical and facts changed with the majority.

explanationZhTw: 「awkward」在這裡形容東西拿起來不順手、不好操作；不是指人尷尬，而是指鉛筆握在手裡不方便。
generatedExample: This box is awkward to carry on the stairs.

explanationZhTw: 「write down」是片語動詞，意思是把想法或資訊寫下來、記錄下來。
generatedExample: Please write down your email address.

explanationZhTw: 「came into his head」是慣用說法，表示某個念頭突然出現在腦中，也就是「突然想到」。
generatedExample: A funny idea came into my head during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「capitals」不是首都，而是指大寫字母；在這個語境中屬於較特殊的用法。
generatedExample: Please type your name in capitals.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》中的黨國口號，屬於刻意顛倒意義的「雙重思想」用法：表面上是矛盾的句子，實際上是在宣傳「真正的自由」會被視為危險或需要被消滅。
generatedExample: In the novel, "FREEDOM IS SLAVERY" shows how the regime twists language to control thought.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的意思不是「幾乎沒有停頓」的字面描述，而是指他幾乎立刻、沒有中斷地繼續動作。
generatedExample: She answered almost without a pause, as if she had rehearsed it.

explanationZhTw: 這是固定說法，字面上是「二加二等於五」，但實際上表示明知錯誤卻硬要宣稱為真，或接受明顯不合理的說法。
generatedExample: In the meeting, he kept insisting that the failed plan was a success, as if two and two made five.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `check` 不是「檢查」，而是指思路突然卡住、停頓一下，像是腦中出現了阻礙。
generatedExample: There was a brief check in my thinking, and I forgot the next step.

explanationZhTw: `shy away from` 表示因為不舒服、害怕或抗拒而避開某事。
generatedExample: She shies away from difficult conversations.

explanationZhTw: `recall` 是「想起、回想起」；這裡指他暫時無法把下一個念頭或內容想起來。
generatedExample: I can recall the first line of the poem, but not the rest.

explanationZhTw: `reasoning out` 是靠一步一步推理把答案想出來，不是自然直接浮現。
generatedExample: We reasoning out the answer from the clues.

explanationZhTw: `come of its own accord` 是慣用語，意思是「自然而然出現、自己冒出來」，不是靠刻意去想。
generatedExample: The solution did not come of its own accord, so we had to work it out.

explanationZhTw: 神；上帝。這是最基本的宗教詞彙，常用來指信仰中的最高神明。
generatedExample: Many people pray to God for guidance.

explanationZhTw: 力量；權力。這裡可理解為支配、控制或影響他人的能力，不只是單純的體力。
generatedExample: The company gained more power in the market.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「可以被改寫、被重新敘述的」，帶有歷史被操控或竄改的意味。
generatedExample: The timeline looked alterable after the new evidence appeared.

explanationZhTw: 法律語境中指「被指控犯有…罪名」。
generatedExample: She was charged with theft after the investigation.

explanationZhTw: 指「證明…是錯的、推翻…的說法」。
generatedExample: One witness disproved the manager’s claim.

explanationZhTw: 指「自我欺騙」，也就是自己說服自己接受不想面對的事實。
generatedExample: Her confidence was built on self-deception rather than facts.

explanationZhTw: 指「投降、放棄抵抗」；這裡也帶有「不再對現實掙扎」的意思。
generatedExample: After hours of debate, he finally chose to surrender.

explanationZhTw: 口語片語，指「轉身、轉向」；在這裡是比喻改變方向或立場。
generatedExample: When she realized the trail was wrong, she decided to turn round.

explanationZhTw: 指「命中註定的、事先已安排好的」。
generatedExample: He acted as if every event was predestined.

explanationZhTw: 指「反抗、造反、抗拒權威」；這裡是對既有秩序或思想的反叛。
generatedExample: The students rebelled against the unfair rules.

explanationZhTw: 表示「所謂的」，帶有一點懷疑或諷刺，意思是這名稱不一定真實或可靠。
generatedExample: The so-called shortcut saved time, but it actually caused more work.

explanationZhTw: 指「自然法則」，是描述自然界規律的固定說法。
generatedExample: Scientists try to explain the laws of Nature with evidence.

explanationZhTw: 指「重力定律／萬有引力定律」，這裡是具體科學術語。
generatedExample: The law of gravity keeps objects from floating away from Earth.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「飄離、浮起離開」；在這裡是字面上的懸浮。
generatedExample: A balloon can float off into the sky if you let go of it.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb，表示「推算出來、想明白」。
generatedExample: After a few minutes, she worked it out and found the answer.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用性的說法，表示一個念頭「突然冒出來」；不是字面上的爆裂。
generatedExample: An old memory burst into his mind while he was walking home.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面動作，而是表示把念頭「壓下去、刻意不去想」。
generatedExample: She pushed the thought under and kept focusing on the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 指「謬誤、錯誤的推論」；是較正式的邏輯詞。
generatedExample: The argument was based on a fallacy, so it did not prove anything.

explanationZhTw: 表示「預設、先假定」某件事是真的，通常是沒有先證明就當作前提。
generatedExample: His question presupposed that everyone already knew the answer.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，這裡表示「處理掉、擺脫」；指把那個想法迅速排除。
generatedExample: We need to dispose of these old reports before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 謬誤；指推理或論證上的錯誤，常是看起來合理但其實不成立的想法。
generatedExample: The argument is built on a simple fallacy.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 succumbing to 表示「屈服於、被...左右」；指最後還是接受了那個想法。
generatedExample: She knew the offer was tempting, but she refused to succumb to it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「發生」，而是「突然想到、進入他的腦中」。
generatedExample: It occurred to me that we had missed an important detail.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，字面是視覺盲點，這裡指「思考上的盲區」或「不自覺的弱點」。
generatedExample: Patience is a blind spot for him when he feels rushed.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的新語術語，指能自動阻止危險念頭的心理機制。
generatedExample: The novel invents CRIMESTOP as a control device for thought.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的「新語」，一種刻意被設計來限制思想的語言。
generatedExample: The government uses Newspeak to make rebellion harder to think about.

explanationZhTw: 開始著手做某事；這裡指他開始進行這項訓練。
generatedExample: She set to work as soon as the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: 刻意訓練自己去掌握某種能力；這裡不是一般的「運動」意思。
generatedExample: He exercises himself in careful listening every day.

explanationZhTw: 《一九八四》中的造詞，指自動停止危險或不被允許的想法。
generatedExample: In the novel, crimestop is a form of self-censorship.

explanationZhTw: 命題、主張；在這裡指要自己接受並處理的政治說法。
generatedExample: The teacher asked us to evaluate the propositions one by one.

explanationZhTw: 訓練自己熟悉或習慣某種做法；這裡指他刻意培養一種思考方式。
generatedExample: She trained herself in keeping calm under pressure.

explanationZhTw: 與……矛盾；表示那些論點和前面的主張相衝突。
generatedExample: His actions contradicted everything he had promised.

explanationZhTw: 推理、理性思考的能力。
generatedExample: Good reasoning helps people reach better decisions.

explanationZhTw: 即興應變；沒有事先準備下臨場發揮。
generatedExample: The band saved the show with some quick improvisation.

explanationZhTw: 算術的；與加減乘除等計算有關。
generatedExample: We solved an arithmetical puzzle in class.

explanationZhTw: 理解能力、知識掌握力；這裡指他無法在腦中真正掌握。
generatedExample: The idea is simple, but it was beyond her intellectual grasp at first.

explanationZhTw: 腦力上的靈活與敏捷；像運動員一樣快速、協調地思考。
generatedExample: Solving that puzzle required real athleticism of mind.

explanationZhTw: 細緻而精準的；這裡指對邏輯非常講究、非常微妙的運用。
generatedExample: She made a delicate adjustment to the plan.

explanationZhTw: 沒有察覺到的；這裡指對明顯錯誤毫無自覺。
generatedExample: He was unconscious of the risk he was taking.

explanationZhTw: 最粗糙、最明顯的；這裡指最顯而易見的邏輯錯誤。
generatedExample: Even the crudest mistake can ruin the result.

explanationZhTw: 達到、獲得；這裡表示某種狀態或能力是努力後才取得的。
generatedExample: It took years to attain that level of skill.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是單純指「開槍射擊」的動作，而是指「把某人槍決／處決」的意思。
generatedExample: The witness thought they would shoot him if he spoke.

explanationZhTw: 指「單獨監禁」，把囚犯長時間單獨關押，不能和其他人接觸。
generatedExample: He spent a month in solitary confinement after the fight.

explanationZhTw: 指「勞改營／勞動營」，通常是強制勞動的拘禁場所。
generatedExample: The prisoners were sent to a labour-camp far from the city.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 drama 不是「戲劇」，而是指一連串戲劇化、令人緊張的事件經過。
generatedExample: She relived the whole drama of the breakup in her mind.

explanationZhTw: 指「審訊、盤問」，特別是警方或軍方對嫌疑人進行正式詢問。
generatedExample: The suspect refused to answer any questions during the interrogation.

explanationZhTw: 表示「再一次、從頭再來」，常用來強調某件事重複發生，帶有不耐煩或厭煩語氣。
generatedExample: We had to start the report all over again after the file was lost.

explanationZhTw: 表示「毫無預警地、突然地」，強調事情發生前沒有任何提示。
generatedExample: The storm hit the coast without warning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `fell into` 不是「掉進去」的字面意思，而是「突然進入某種狀態」，例如陷入夢境、沉思或情緒裡。
generatedExample: I fell into a daydream while listening to the lecture.

explanationZhTw: `reverie` 指「出神、白日夢、恍惚的幻想狀態」，帶有安靜、飄忽的感覺。
generatedExample: She sat by the window in a pleasant reverie.

explanationZhTw: 這是帶有隱喻的說法，表示「等待即將到來的致命結果或災難」，不是字面上真的在等一顆子彈。
generatedExample: After the scandal broke, he felt like he was waiting for the bullet.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `settled` 是「事情已經定案、解決妥當」的意思，不是「坐下」或「安定居住」。
generatedExample: Once the details were settled, we could start the project.

explanationZhTw: `smoothed out` 表示把麻煩、衝突或不順的地方「理順、解決、變平順」。
generatedExample: They smoothed out the last problems before launch.

explanationZhTw: `reconciled` 在這裡表示「和解、調和、變得彼此不衝突」，強調矛盾已經消失。
generatedExample: After the argument, the two friends reconciled.

explanationZhTw: `delirium` 是「譫妄、神志錯亂」的醫學或文學用語，指意識混亂、判斷失常的狀態。
generatedExample: The patient was talking wildly in a delirium.

explanationZhTw: `induced` 這裡表示「由……引起、造成」，不是一般的「說服」意思。
generatedExample: The symptoms were induced by stress.

explanationZhTw: `rabbit-cropped` 是形容草地被兔子啃食得很短、很整齊；屬於很具畫面感的特殊形容法。
generatedExample: We crossed a rabbit-cropped field near the river.

explanationZhTw: `dace` 是一種小型淡水魚，屬於較少見的具體名詞。
generatedExample: Small dace darted through the clear water.

explanationZhTw: 「started up」在這裡不是「開始」，而是指因驚嚇而猛然一動、突然坐起或警覺地抬起身體。
generatedExample: He started up when he heard the sudden noise.

explanationZhTw: 「broke out」在這裡表示汗水突然冒出來，屬於描述身體反應的片語，不是「爆發衝突」那種常見用法。
generatedExample: Sweat broke out on his face during the interview.

explanationZhTw: 「aloud」表示「出聲地、放聲地」，強調真的說出口，而不是在心裡默念。
generatedExample: Please read the paragraph aloud.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `my love` 不是字面上的「我的愛」，而是對親密對象的稱呼，帶有強烈的呼喚與情感色彩，類似中文的「親愛的」。
generatedExample: "My love, please wait for me."

explanationZhTw: 形容感受或程度非常強烈、讓人難以承受；這裡指那個幻覺強烈到幾乎壓倒他。
generatedExample: She felt an overwhelming sense of relief when the test was over.

explanationZhTw: 「幻覺」；在這裡指他不是實際看見她，而是產生了非常真切的感知錯覺。
generatedExample: After staying awake for two days, he started having hallucinations.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「出席」，而是指「在場感、存在感」；也就是他強烈感到她似乎就在身邊。
generatedExample: Even after she left the room, her presence still seemed to fill it.

explanationZhTw: phrasal verb「get into」在這裡是「滲入、進入」的意思；用比喻表達她像滲進他的身體和感受裡。
generatedExample: The cold wind got into my clothes and made me shiver.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是比喻用法，不是單純指皮膚的物理「紋理」；意思是她彷彿深入到他最內在、最表層的感官與身體感受中。
generatedExample: The rough texture of the stone made it hard to hold.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「某個地方、但說不太準確」；帶有模糊、不確定的語氣。
generatedExample: I left my keys somewhere or other, but I can't remember where.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `lay back` 是「向後躺、往後靠」的意思，屬於常見的片語動詞用法。
generatedExample: She lay back on the sofa and closed her eyes for a minute.

explanationZhTw: `compose himself` 在這裡不是「作曲」，而是指「讓自己冷靜下來、恢復鎮定」。
generatedExample: After the argument, he took a deep breath to compose himself.

explanationZhTw: `servitude` 指「奴役、被迫服從的狀態」，在這裡帶有強烈的政治或人生壓迫感，語氣比日常用語更正式。
generatedExample: The workers lived in servitude for generations before the laws changed.

explanationZhTw: `moment of weakness` 是常見說法，指「一時軟弱、一時失控或做了不該做的事的短暫時刻」。
generatedExample: I bought the expensive jacket in a moment of weakness.

explanationZhTw: 指「沉重的腳步聲」，這裡用來營造外面有人走近的緊張感。
generatedExample: From the hallway, we heard the tramp of boots before the door opened.

explanationZhTw: 指「突然爆發的一陣情緒或言語」，通常帶有失控、激烈的意思。
generatedExample: His outburst surprised everyone in the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「不受懲罰、放過不罰」。
generatedExample: If he steals again, the crime should not go unpunished.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般商業合約，而是指他和對方之間的某種約定或默契。
generatedExample: She broke the agreement by telling other people the secret.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是專有的政治用法，指統治一切的政黨或政權，不是一般「派對」。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Party controls almost every part of life.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是專有的政治用法，指統治一切的政黨或政權，不是一般「派對」。
generatedExample: In the novel, the Party controls almost every part of life.

explanationZhTw: 指「異端的、違反正統思想的」，這裡是對官方思想不忠、不合教條的意思。
generatedExample: At that time, even a heretical idea could bring serious punishment.

explanationZhTw: 指「順從、符合規範」，也就是外表上表現得跟大家一樣。
generatedExample: The school rewarded conformity instead of independent thinking.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「撤退」的軍事動作，而是比喻他在心理上又退讓了一步。
generatedExample: After the argument, she retreated into silence.

explanationZhTw: 指「投降、屈服」，這裡是比喻在思想上放棄抵抗。
generatedExample: He surrendered after realizing he had no way to win.

explanationZhTw: 指「未受侵犯的、保持完整無損的」，語氣正式，帶有保護與不可侵犯的意味。
generatedExample: They wanted to keep their privacy inviolate.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「有錯、理虧」。
generatedExample: I knew I was in the wrong, so I apologized first.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「有錯、理虧」。
generatedExample: I knew I was in the wrong, so I apologized first.

explanationZhTw: 指「承認、坦白」，這裡表示他那一聲喊叫把心裡的真相全暴露出來。
generatedExample: She confessed that she had broken the vase.

explanationZhTw: 表示「從頭再來、重新開始一次」。
generatedExample: After the setback, we had to start all over again.

explanationZhTw: 表示讓自己熟悉或逐漸適應某件事。
generatedExample: She read the manual to familiarize herself with the software.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `glass` 不是「玻璃」，而是指「鏡子」。
generatedExample: He checked his reflection in the glass before going out.

explanationZhTw: 指「難以看透、神情深不可測」的狀態。
generatedExample: Her inscrutability made everyone at the table uneasy.

explanationZhTw: 表示「一直同時地、始終如此」；強調某事在整段時間內持續發生。
generatedExample: He smiled politely, all the while planning his exit.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「把……關住／壓抑住、藏起來」。
generatedExample: He kept his anger locked up and said nothing.

explanationZhTw: `shoot` 在這裡是「開槍射殺」，不是一般的「射擊」動作；整句表示他終有一天會被人決定處決。
generatedExample: They threatened to shoot him if he came any closer.

explanationZhTw: `beforehand` 表示「事先、提前」，這裡強調只能在極短時間前預知。
generatedExample: Please let me know beforehand if you will be late.

explanationZhTw: `from behind` 表示「從背後、從後方」，用來描述攻擊來自背面，帶有偷襲、無法防備的意味。
generatedExample: Someone tapped me from behind, and I turned around quickly.

explanationZhTw: `corridor` 是「走廊」；這裡的畫面感很強，表示他正走在狹長通道中，容易被從背後接近。
generatedExample: We walked down a corridor lined with old photographs.

explanationZhTw: `turn over` 在這裡不是單純「翻轉物體」，而是比喻心境、意識在短時間內徹底翻轉或改變。
generatedExample: Her thoughts began to turn over as she waited for the result.

explanationZhTw: `without a word uttered` 是較文學性的說法，意思是「一句話都沒說」；`utter` 在這裡是「說出」。
generatedExample: She left the room without a word uttered.

explanationZhTw: `camouflage` 原本是「偽裝、掩飾」，這裡指他外表的冷靜與假面具被卸下。
generatedExample: His cheerful smile was just camouflage for his anxiety.

explanationZhTw: `would go` 在這裡不是單純「會去」，而是帶有突然發作、突然啟動的效果，配合前面的擬聲詞 `bang!`。
generatedExample: When she heard the news, all her composure would go at once.

explanationZhTw: `fill` 在這裡是比喻用法，表示憤怒或仇恨「充滿」整個人，強調情緒完全占據他。
generatedExample: Fear filled him before the exam started.

explanationZhTw: `in the same instant` 表示「幾乎在同一瞬間」，強調兩件事發生得非常接近。
generatedExample: In the same instant, both phones started ringing.

explanationZhTw: `out of their reach` 是慣用說法，表示「超出他們能控制或抓到的範圍」。
generatedExample: The password is out of their reach unless they know the backup code.

explanationZhTw: `blown a hole in` 在這裡是比喻，表示「破壞、戳破」某種自以為完整無缺的狀態。
generatedExample: One small mistake blew a hole in their perfect plan.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「使自己墮落、貶低自己的尊嚴」，不是一般的「降級」。
generatedExample: He felt that lying to his friends was degrading himself.

explanationZhTw: 指「自我殘害、傷害自己的身心」；語氣非常強烈，帶有身體或精神上的毀損感。
generatedExample: He described the punishment as mutilating himself for a mistake he had not made.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是片語動詞，表示「一頭陷入、迫使自己投入」某種可怕或骯髒的情境。
generatedExample: She had to plunge into the paperwork after the holiday.

explanationZhTw: 這是強烈的誇飾說法，意思是「最骯髒、最污穢的東西」；重點在情緒性的厭惡感。
generatedExample: They were forced to work in the filthiest of filth during the storm.

explanationZhTw: 表示「來來回回、往返地」；這裡用來描寫眼睛隨著你左右移動。
generatedExample: The children ran to and fro across the yard.

explanationZhTw: 表示「自然而然地、自己就發生了」，強調不是他刻意想到，而是形象自己浮現。
generatedExample: The memory came back of its own accord when she heard the song.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `tramp` 不是「流浪漢」，而是指一連串沉重的腳步聲或踏步聲，屬於偏文學性的用法。
generatedExample: We heard the tramp of soldiers marching down the street.

explanationZhTw: `passage` 在這裡是「走廊、通道」的意思，不是一般的「段落」。
generatedExample: The nurse waited in the passage outside the room.

explanationZhTw: `swung open` 表示門「猛然打開、甩開打開」，是個常見的片語動詞式用法。
generatedExample: The front gate swung open as soon as I pushed it.

explanationZhTw: `clang` 是金屬碰撞時發出的「鏗然聲、叮噹聲」，這裡用來描寫鐵門打開時的刺耳聲響。
generatedExample: The pan fell to the floor with a loud clang.

explanationZhTw: `cell` 在這裡指「牢房」，是監禁空間的專門用法。
generatedExample: The prisoner was taken back to his cell after dinner.

explanationZhTw: `waxen-faced` 是文學性的形容，意思是「臉色像蠟一樣蒼白、毫無血色」，帶有陰冷感。
generatedExample: After the long illness, he looked waxen-faced and tired.

explanationZhTw: 「get up」是常見的片語動詞，意思是「起床、起身」。在這裡是命令語氣，要求對方立刻站起來或離開床/座位。
generatedExample: I usually get up at 7 a.m. on weekdays.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「opposite」表示「在對面、面對著」，是描述位置的常用字；不是「相反的」那種意思。
generatedExample: She stood opposite the door and waited for him to open it.

explanationZhTw: 「shoulders」是「肩膀」，屬於基礎身體部位詞；這裡用來描述他握住溫斯頓雙肩的動作。
generatedExample: He put both hands on her shoulders to calm her down.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「closely」表示「仔細地、近距離地」，不是「親密地」；用來描述他專注地觀察對方。
generatedExample: The doctor looked at the X-ray closely before speaking.

explanationZhTw: deceive 意為「欺騙、蒙騙」；這裡是指「你曾想要騙我」。
generatedExample: She accused him of deceiving her about the money.

explanationZhTw: stupid 是常見形容詞，意思是「愚蠢的、笨的」；這裡是在責罵對方剛才的想法或行為。
generatedExample: It was stupid to leave the door unlocked.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純描述「看我的臉」，而是帶有命令和施壓語氣，表示要對方正面直視自己，不准閃避。
generatedExample: If you are telling the truth, look me in the face and say it again.

explanationZhTw: 「paused」指暫停動作或說話片刻，這裡是很常見的動詞用法，表示他先停了一下。
generatedExample: She paused before answering the question.

explanationZhTw: 「went on」在這裡是片語動詞，意思是「繼續說下去」或「接著做下去」，不是指「走上去」。
generatedExample: After a short break, the speaker went on with her story.

explanationZhTw: 「tone」在這裡不是指聲音的音調高低，而是指說話的語氣、口氣或態度。
generatedExample: His tone became calmer when he realized I was upset.

explanationZhTw: 現在分詞/形容詞用法，表示「正在進步、變得更好」。
generatedExample: Your pronunciation is improving every week.

explanationZhTw: 指「在智力上、理性上」；這裡是在區分腦力層面和情緒層面。
generatedExample: Intellectually, the plan makes sense, but it still feels risky.

explanationZhTw: 指「在情感上、就情緒而言」；和感受、心情有關。
generatedExample: She was emotionally tired after the long argument.

explanationZhTw: 指「察覺、識破謊言」；detect 是辨認出某件事。
generatedExample: The officer could detect a lie from his nervous voice.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「真正的感覺」，而是指你對「老大哥」的真實態度、忠誠或內心立場。
generatedExample: He would never say his true feelings towards the new rules out loud.

explanationZhTw: 「hate」表示強烈的厭惡或憎恨，是很常見的基本動詞。
generatedExample: I hate waking up early on Mondays.

explanationZhTw: 表示「時候到了／現在正是時機」，不是字面上「時間到來」而已。
generatedExample: The time has come to make a decision.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純的「走最後一步」，而是指完成最後、最關鍵的一步。
generatedExample: After months of planning, she finally took the last step and launched the business.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 love 不是一般的「喜歡／愛」，而是指被灌輸後對政權的忠誠與服從。
generatedExample: In the story, citizens are told to love the leader without question.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `released` 不是「釋放」某人離開監禁，而是指「把抓住的人放開／鬆手讓他離開控制」。
generatedExample: The officer released the child and stepped back.

explanationZhTw: 「Room 101」在《一九八四》裡是帶有特殊象徵意義的專名，指的是讓人面對最深恐懼的房間，不是一般字面上的「101號房間」。
generatedExample: For him, the final interview felt like Room 101, where every fear was suddenly real.
